text: Emergency crews are preparing to work throughout the weekend to contain the biggest oil spill in the history of the existing Keystone pipeline. Nearly six hundred thousand gallons of oil have gushed out of the pipeline in rural Kansas over the last few days. The Canadian company that operates Keystone says it is still investigating the cause of the breach. The pipeline runs from Canada to Oklahoma, usually carrying more than twenty-six million gallons of oil each day. Hey, thanks for watching! Don't miss the Today Show every weekday at eleven a.m. Eastern, eight Pacific, on our streaming channel today all day. To watch, head to today.com/allday or click the link right here.
